tok: aor pass ptcp nom sg m
lit: And in the lamb, having been slaughtered,
sm: And in the lamb, having been slaughtered,

syn.mc: Participial phrase in the descriptive series.
syn.svo: Implicit referent: Christ. Participle: σφαγείς. Prepositional phrase: ἐν δὲ τῷ ἀμνῷ.
syn.ptc: Circumstantial participle describing the sacrificial mode.
syn.rhet: The lamb image is the first predicate in a climactic triad.

tok: aor pass ptcp nom sg m
lit: And in David, having been persecuted,
sm: And in David, having been persecuted,

syn.mc: Participial phrase in the descriptive series.
syn.svo: Implicit referent: Christ. Participle: διωχθείς. Prepositional phrase: ἐν δὲ τῷ Δαυεὶδ.
syn.ptc: Circumstantial participle describing the Davidic pattern.
syn.rhet: The sequence extends from sacrificial image to royal persecution.

tok: aor pass ptcp nom sg m
lit: And in the prophets, having been dishonored.
sm: And in the prophets, having been dishonored.

syn.mc: Final participial member of the threefold series.
syn.svo: Implicit referent: Christ. Participle: ἀτιμασθείς. Prepositional phrase: ἐν δὲ τοῖς προφήταις.
syn.rhet: The triad spans lamb, David, and the prophets.

src: οὗτός ἐστιν ὁ ἐν παρθένῳ σαρκωθείς,
tok: aor pass ptcp nom sg m
lit: This is the one incarnate in a virgin,
sm: This is the one made flesh in a virgin,

syn.svo: Subject: οὗτός. Verb: ἐστιν. Predicate participle: ὁ ... σαρκωθείς. Locative phrase: ἐν παρθένῳ.
syn.rhet: The anaphoric formula turns the descriptive series into christological identification.

tok: aor pass ptcp nom sg m
lit: the one hanged on wood,
sm: the one hanged on the tree,

syn.svo: Implied referent: Christ. Participle: κρεμασθείς. Prepositional phrase: ἐπὶ ξύλου.
syn.ptc: Further descriptive participle in the same appositional chain.
syn.rhet: The cross image enters the same enumerative sequence.

tok: aor pass ptcp nom sg m
lit: the one buried into the earth,
sm: the one buried in the earth,

syn.svo: Implied referent: Christ. Participle: ταφείς. Prepositional phrase: εἰς γῆν.
syn.ptc: A further member of the burial/resurrection sequence.
syn.rhet: The descent into earth deepens the burial imagery.

tok: aor pass ptcp nom sg m
lit: the one raised from the dead,
sm: the one raised from the dead,
emd: Corrected the transmitted ὃ to ὁ; the substantival participle requires the article.

syn.svo: Implied referent: Christ. Participle: ἀνασταθείς. Prepositional phrase: ἐκ νεκρῶν.
syn.ptc: Resurrection is expressed with the same substantival participial pattern.
syn.rhet: The burial sequence turns toward resurrection.

src: ὁ εἰς τὰ ὑψηλὰ τῶν οὐρανῶν ἀναλημφθείς.
tok: aor pass ptcp nom sg m
lit: the one taken up into the heights of the heavens.
sm: the one taken up into the heights of heaven.

syn.mc: Final appositive participle in the ascending series.
syn.svo: Implied referent: Christ. Participle: ἀναλημφθείς. Prepositional phrase: εἰς τὰ ὑψηλὰ τῶν οὐρανῶν.
syn.ptc: The sequence now moves upward after burial and resurrection.
syn.rhet: The triad of burial, rising, and ascension is complete.

src: οὗτός ἐστιν ὃ ἀμνὸς ὁ φονευόμενος"
ana: οὗτός ἐστιν ὁ ἀμνὸς ὁ φονευόμενος.
tok: pres mp ptcp nom sg m
lit: This is the lamb being slain.
sm: This is the lamb being slain.
emd: Corrected transmitted ὃ to ὁ and normalized the trailing OCR quote as punctuation.

syn.svo: Subject: οὗτός. Verb: ἐστιν. Predicate: ὁ ἀμνός. Participial modifier: ὁ φονευόμενος.
syn.rhet: The sacrificial title is stated directly after the participial series.

src: οὗτός ἐστιν ὃ ἀμνὸς ὁ ἄφωνος"
ana: οὗτός ἐστιν ὁ ἀμνὸς ὁ ἄφωνος.
lit: This is the voiceless lamb.
sm: This is the silent lamb.
emd: Corrected transmitted ὃ to ὁ and normalized the trailing OCR quote as punctuation.

syn.svo: Subject: οὗτός. Verb: ἐστιν. Predicate noun: ὁ ἀμνός. Appositive adjective: ὁ ἄφωνος.
syn.rhet: A second direct christological title mirrors the previous line.

src: οὗτός ἐστιν ὁ τεχθεὶς ἐϊς Μαρίας τῆς καλῆς ἀμνάδος"
ana: οὗτός ἐστιν ὁ τεχθεὶς ἐκ Μαρίας τῆς καλῆς ἀμνάδος.
tok: aor pass ptcp nom sg m
lit: This is the one born from Mary, the noble ewe.
sm: This is the one born from Mary, the noble ewe.
emd: Followed change_log.txt: ἐϊς -> ἐκ; normalized the trailing OCR quote as punctuation.

syn.mc: Identification clause with a genitive source phrase.
syn.svo: Subject: οὗτός. Verb: ἐστιν. Predicate participle: ὁ τεχθείς. Source phrase: ἐκ Μαρίας τῆς καλῆς ἀμνάδος.
syn.rhet: Mary is introduced through the sacrificial ewe image.

src: οὗτός ἐστιν ὅ ἐξ ἀγέλης λημφθείς,
ana: οὗτός ἐστιν ὁ ἐξ ἀγέλης λημφθείς,
tok: aor pass ptcp nom sg m
lit: This is the one taken out of the flock,
sm: This is the one taken from the flock,
emd: Corrected transmitted ὅ to ὁ; the substantival participle requires the article.

syn.mc: Identification clause introducing the next participial sequence.
syn.svo: Subject: οὗτός. Verb: ἐστιν. Predicate participle: ὁ ... λημφθείς. Source phrase: ἐξ ἀγέλης.
syn.rhet: The flock image bridges into the slaughter sequence.

tok: aor pass ptcp nom sg m
lit: and dragged into slaughter,
sm: and dragged to slaughter,

syn.svo: Implicit referent: the lamb/Christ. Participle: συρείς. Goal phrase: εἰς σφαγὴν.
syn.ptc: The movement toward slaughter is expressed by the passive participle.
syn.rhet: The sacrificial victim is now led toward death.

tok: aor pass ptcp nom sg m
lit: and sacrificed at evening,
sm: and sacrificed in the evening,

syn.svo: Implicit referent: the lamb/Christ. Participle: τυθείς. Temporal phrase: ἑσπέρας.
syn.ptc: The evening sacrifice evokes Passover timing.
syn.rhet: The sequence aligns death with the liturgical hour.

tok: aor pass ptcp nom sg m
lit: and buried by night,
sm: and buried by night,

syn.svo: Implicit referent: the lamb/Christ. Participle: ταφείς. Adverb: νύκτωρ.
syn.ptc: Night burial keeps the sacrificial rhythm compact.
syn.rhet: The burial is compressed into the darkness of night.

tok: aor pass ptcp nom sg m
lit: the one not broken on the wood,
sm: the one not broken on the wood,
emd: Normalized the trailing OCR quote as punctuation.

syn.svo: Implied referent: Christ. Participle: συντριβείς. Negative marker: μὴ. Prepositional phrase: ἐπὶ ξύλου.
syn.ptc: The negated participle preserves the passover-typological emphasis on unbroken integrity.
syn.rhet: The wood/cross is introduced without rupture of the victim.

tok: aor pass ptcp nom sg m
lit: not dissolved into the earth,
sm: not dissolved into the earth,

syn.svo: Implicit referent: Christ. Participle: λυθείς. Negative marker: μὴ. Prepositional phrase: εἰς γῆν.
syn.ptc: The negative continues the preservation motif.
syn.rhet: The body is not allowed to fall apart into the soil.

tok: aor act ptcp nom sg m
lit: having risen from the dead,
sm: having risen from the dead,

syn.svo: Implicit referent: Christ. Participle: ἀναστάς. Prepositional phrase: ἐκ νεκρῶν.
syn.ptc: The resurrection verb breaks the burial sequence.
syn.rhet: Death is crossed and reversed.

src: καὶ ἀναστήσας τὸν ἄνθρωπον ἐκ τῆς κάτω ταφῆς.
tok: aor act ptcp nom sg m
lit: and having raised man from the lower grave.
sm: and raising man from the grave below.

syn.mc: Final coordinate participial phrase in the sequence.
syn.svo: Implicit subject: Christ. Participle: ἀναστήσας. Object: τὸν ἄνθρωπον. Source phrase: ἐκ τῆς κάτω ταφῆς.
syn.ptc: The culminating participle makes the restorative action explicit.
syn.rhet: The sequence ends with the restoration of humanity.

lit: This one has been murdered.
sm: This one has been murdered.
emd: Normalized the trailing OCR quote as period punctuation.

syn.svo: Subject: Οὗτος. Verb: πεφόνευται.
syn.rhet: A blunt summary sentence introduces the ensuing questions.

ana: καὶ ποῦ πεφόνευται; ἐν μέσῳ Ἰερουσαλήμ.
lit: And where has he been murdered? In the midst of Jerusalem.
sm: And where was he murdered? In the midst of Jerusalem.

syn.mc: Interrogative question followed by a locative answer.
syn.svo: Subject: implied οὗτος. Verb: πεφόνευται. Interrogative adverb: ποῦ. Locative answer: ἐν μέσῳ Ἰερουσαλήμ.
syn.rhet: The question-answer form sharpens the accusation.

lit: By whom? By Israel.
sm: By whom? By Israel.
emd: Followed change_log.txt: ζὑπὸ -> ὑπὸ; removed the stray spacing psili from ᾿Ισραήλ.

syn.mc: Interrogative question followed by a terse answer.
syn.svo: Prepositional question: ὑπὸ τίνων. Answer phrase: ὑπὸ τοῦ Ἰσραήλ.
syn.rhet: The question shifts blame from place to agent.

src: διὰ τί; ὅτι τοὺς χωλοὺς αὐτῶν ἐθεράπευσεν
lit: Why? Because he healed their lame ones
sm: Why? Because he healed their lame ones,

syn.mc: Interrogative answer phrase introducing a causal clause.
syn.svo: Subject: implied he. Verb: ἐθεράπευσεν. Object: τοὺς χωλοὺς αὐτῶν.
syn.rhet: The first benefaction in the list answers the death accusation.

lit: and he cleansed their lepers
sm: and he cleansed their lepers,

syn.mc: Coordinate clause in the causal list.
syn.sub: Dependent on the causal ὅτι-clause in line 545.
syn.svo: Subject: implied he. Verb: ἐκαθάρισεν. Object: τοὺς λεπροὺς αὐτῶν.
syn.rhet: Healing continues as parallel benefaction.

lit: and he gave light to their blind
sm: and he gave sight to their blind,

syn.mc: Coordinate clause in the causal list.
syn.sub: Dependent on the causal ὅτι-clause in line 545.
syn.svo: Subject: implied he. Verb: ἐφωταγώγησεν. Object: τοὺς τυφλοὺς αὐτῶν.
syn.rhet: The list moves from purification to illumination.

lit: and he raised their dead,
sm: and he raised their dead,

syn.mc: Coordinate clause in the causal list.
syn.sub: Dependent on the causal ὅτι-clause in line 545.
syn.svo: Subject: implied he. Verb: ἀνέστησεν. Object: τοὺς νεκροὺς αὐτῶν.
syn.rhet: The final benefaction reaches the dead themselves.

lit: For this reason he died.
sm: For this reason he died.

syn.svo: Subject: implied he. Verb: ἀπέθανεν. Causal phrase: διὰ τοῦτο.
syn.rhet: The healing list paradoxically explains the death sentence.

src: ποῦ γέγραπται ἐν νόμῳ καὶ ἐν προφήταις"
ana: ποῦ γέγραπται ἐν νόμῳ καὶ ἐν προφήταις;
lit: Where has it been written in the law and in the prophets?
sm: Where is it written in the law and in the prophets?
emd: Normalized the trailing OCR quote as question punctuation.

syn.svo: Subject: implied τοῦτο / the cited matter. Verb: γέγραπται. Locative phrases: ἐν νόμῳ καὶ ἐν προφήταις.
syn.rhet: The question turns from accusation to scriptural warrant.
